question: Which algorithm finds shortest paths in an unweighted graph using a queue?
title: Graph Traversal with BFS
text: Breadth-first search explores a graph level by level using a queue. BFS starts from a source node, marks it visited, and repeatedly pops the front of the queue to visit all unvisited neighbors. In unweighted graphs, BFS also computes shortest-path distances measured in the number of edges.

question: Which algorithm finds shortest paths in an unweighted graph using a queue?
title: Shortest Paths with Dijkstra's Algorithm
text: Dijkstra's algorithm solves the single-source shortest path problem on graphs with nonnegative edge weights. It repeatedly extracts the node with the smallest tentative distance from a priority queue and relaxes outgoing edges. Unlike BFS, Dijkstra handles weighted graphs.

question: Which algorithm finds shortest paths in an unweighted graph using a queue?
title: CPU Scheduling Basics
text: CPU scheduling policies decide which ready process runs next. Common policies include first-come first-served, shortest job first, priority scheduling, and round robin. Round robin uses a time quantum to improve responsiveness for interactive workloads.

question: Which algorithm finds shortest paths in an unweighted graph using a queue?
title: TCP Reliable Transport
text: Transmission Control Protocol provides reliable, ordered, byte-stream delivery between endpoints. TCP uses acknowledgments, sequence numbers, retransmission, and flow control to ensure reliable transport over an unreliable IP network.

question: Which algorithm finds shortest paths in an unweighted graph using a queue?
title: Backpropagation in Neural Networks
text: Backpropagation computes gradients of the loss function with respect to network parameters by applying the chain rule from output layers back to input layers. Gradient descent or its variants then update weights using these gradients during training.

question: What causes a page fault in virtual memory?
title: Paging and Virtual Memory
text: Paging divides virtual memory into fixed-size pages and physical memory into frames. A page table maps virtual page numbers to frame numbers. When a referenced page is not in memory, a page fault occurs and the operating system loads the page from secondary storage.

question: How can we describe underfitting and overfitting using bias and variance?
title: Bias-Variance Trade-off
text: The bias-variance trade-off explains model generalization. High-bias models underfit because they are too simple, while high-variance models overfit training data and fail to generalize. Regularization, more data, and model selection help balance bias and variance.
